bead.title: Wire required execution gates into ddx agent execute-bead (interactive path)
bead.description: Interactive path in cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (lines ~189-195) explicitly clears WtPath/GovernIDs in BeadLandingOptions with comment "worktree has been cleaned up by ExecuteBead." Pick one of two designs: (i) defer worktree cleanup so the orchestrator owns it after gate eval, or (ii) re-checkout an ephemeral worktree at ResultRev before LandBeadResult and prune it after. Plumb GovernIDs from the worker manifest via ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest. evaluateRequiredGates already fires correctly inside LandBeadResult when WtPath and GovernIDs are non-empty (execute_bead_orchestrator.go:220).
bead.acceptance:
- [ ] ddx agent execute-bead populates BeadLandingOptions.WtPath and GovernIDs when manifest declares governing IDs
- [ ] A required failing gate preserves the attempt under refs/ddx/iterations/, writes gate results into the execution bundle, and surfaces post_run_check_failed in the result
- [ ] A required passing gate still merges and records the pass summary
- [ ] Regression test in cli/internal/agent/ covers required-pass and required-fail fixtures driven through the interactive code path
- [ ] No-gate case still merges (backward compatible)
